dry_bulk.confluence_note: While spot indices are in a technical expansion regime with the BDI at the 99.5th percentile, qualitative signals have soured with negative fundamentals and a neutral sentiment shift.
dry_bulk.summary: Capesize volatility has collapsed to multi-year lows despite high spot rates, as the market begins pricing in a significant second-half slowdown in Asia. High Chinese iron ore portside inventories (up 21.9% YOY) and falling steel production (-4.5% YTD) suggest the current price strength lacks fundamental longevity.
dry_bulk.outlook: Cautious; high spot rates are increasingly vulnerable to a 2H 2026 demand cliff.
dry_bulk.watch: China iron ore inventory levels and potential further contraction in steel output.

tanker.confluence_note: Extreme quantitative expansion in clean and dirty segments is strongly supported by positive fundamental outlooks tied to geopolitical disruptions.
tanker.summary: Clean tanker rates have seen a parabolic 143.9% 60-day ROC, driven by unnavigable straits and supply chain adjustments. Although momentum recently softened to negative as the market absorbs the initial 'panic bid,' fundamentals remain robust due to inventory draws and structural shifts in trade routes. Segment breadth remains important: clean tankers at 2178.0 (ROC60 +143.9%), alongside dirty tankers at 2811.0 (ROC60 +70.0%).
key_signals: Elevated oil prices and Middle East volatility
tanker.outlook: Bullish but volatile; supply-side constraints remain the primary price driver.
tanker.watch: Navigation status of major straits and OECD crude stock levels.

macro_note: A stark bifurcation is emerging as geopolitical risk drives tanker markets to extreme highs, while dry bulk faces mounting macro headwinds from an anticipated Asian industrial slowdown in the latter half of the year.
